verses.text: When the LORD restored Zion's captives, 'we were like them that dream,' their mouths filled with laughter; even the heathen owned, 'The LORD hath done great things for them.' Deliverance seemed too good to be true. God's surprising mercies turn sorrow into songs of joy.

When the LORD restored Zion's captives, 'we were like them that dream,' their mouths filled with laughter; even the heathen owned, 'The LORD hath done great things for them.' Deliverance seemed too good to be true. God's surprising mercies turn sorrow into songs of joy.

When the LORD restored Zion's captives, 'we were like them that dream,' their mouths filled with laughter; even the heathen owned, 'The LORD hath done great things for them.' Deliverance seemed too good to be true. God's surprising mercies turn sorrow into songs of joy.

They pray, 'Turn again our captivity,' confident that 'they that sow in tears shall reap in joy,' returning with sheaves. Present grief shall yield a glad harvest. The tears of God's people are seed that ripens into rejoicing.
